name: Chocolate-Filled Holiday Balls
recipeIngredient: 1 cup (2 sticks) salted butter or margarine, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
3. In a food processor, pulse walnut pieces and 2/3 cup confectioners' sugar until finely ground.
4. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour and remaining ground walnut mixture.
5. Beat butter, remaining confectioners' sugar, and vanilla in an electric mixer until creamy (about 2-3 minutes).
6. Mix in the flour mixture until just combined.
7. Scoop dough into heaping teaspoons onto prepared sheets, spacing about 2 inches apart.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden around edges.
9. Cool cookies completely on wire racks.
10. For filling: melt chocolate and butter in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Add cream, vanilla, then confectioners' sugar to taste. Set aside.
11. Assemble cookies by spreading filling onto one side of each cooled cookie. Top with another cookie flat side down.
12. Roll edges into walnut mixture.
13. Store in an airtight container, separating layers with waxed paper.
Yield: Approximately 18 sandwich cookies
